page: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpldsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 3780 GB of bundled local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage. The processor features a 128 MB L3 cache and hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks x86-specific vector extensions such as AVX or AMX.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64plds_v6 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance and memory subsystem efficiency. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests place the server in the top 10% of tested instances, driven by its 64 dedicated physical cores. Memory bandwidth is a primary strength, with cached small-block reads, uncached large-block reads, and large-block writes all registering in the top 10% tier. This high memory throughput supports rapid data processing, as evidenced by top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark database operations. Single-core performance for tasks like Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core remains in the average tier.

The primary hardware tradeoff of the D64plds_v6 is its low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive in-memory databases but enhances its qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads. By pairing high-density ARM compute cores with a substantial 3780 GB local storage allocation, the instance offers a balanced cost-to-performance ratio for scale-out architectures. The server is optimized for workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves top-tier throughput, as well as software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, and database operations that leverage its high multi-core capacity and fast memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dpldsv6 family, featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 3780 GB of bundled local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, placing in the top 10% for multi-threaded benchmarks, software compilation, and database operations. However, single-core performance and memory capacity per core are moderate. This resource profile makes the server qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, scale-out workloads. It is highly suited for static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and database workloads that benefit from high core density and fast local storage without requiring massive memory footprints.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v6 is a 64-core ARM Neoverse-N2 virtual machine with 128 GB RAM and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v6 is an ARM-based 64-core virtual machine with 128 GB RAM and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered 64-core server combining high-density compute, fast memory bandwidth, and substantial local storage for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
128 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Bundled 3780 GB local storage with Premium Storage support
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
Well-suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
